title: Actions System
description: Standard actions available to characters and monsters during gameplay

overview.description: When you do something other than moving or communicating, you typically take an action
overview.reference: The Action table lists the game's main actions, which are defined in more detail in the Rules Glossary
overview.flexibility: Player characters and monsters can also do things not covered by these actions. Many class features and other abilities provide additional action options, and you can improvise other actions.

standard_actions.name: Attack
standard_actions.summary: Attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike
standard_actions.description: Make one or more weapon attacks or unarmed strikes against targets within range

standard_actions.name: Dash
standard_actions.summary: For the rest of the turn, give yourself extra movement equal to your Speed
standard_actions.description: Double your movement for the turn by gaining additional movement equal to your current Speed

standard_actions.name: Disengage
standard_actions.summary: Your movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks for the rest of the turn
standard_actions.description: Move away from enemies without triggering opportunity attacks

standard_actions.name: Dodge
standard_actions.summary: Until the start of your next turn, attack rolls against you have Disadvantage, and you make Dexterity saving throws with Advantage. You lose this benefit if you have the Incapacitated condition or if your Speed is 0
standard_actions.description: Focus entirely on avoiding attacks and area effects

standard_actions.name: Help
standard_actions.summary: Help another creature's ability check or attack roll, or administer first aid
standard_actions.description: Assist an ally with their next attempt or provide basic medical care

standard_actions.name: Hide
standard_actions.description: Attempt to become hidden from enemies by using cover or concealment

standard_actions.name: Influence
standard_actions.summary: Make a Charisma (Deception, Intimidation, Performance, or Persuasion) or Wisdom (Animal Handling) check to alter a creature's attitude
standard_actions.description: Use social skills to change how creatures react to you or your party

standard_actions.name: Magic
standard_actions.summary: Cast a spell, use a magic item, or use a magical feature
standard_actions.description: Activate any magical ability, whether from spells, items, or class features

standard_actions.name: Ready
standard_actions.summary: Prepare to take an action in response to a trigger you define
standard_actions.description: Hold your action until a specific condition occurs, then react immediately

standard_actions.name: Search
standard_actions.summary: Make a Wisdom (Insight, Medicine, Perception, or Survival) check
standard_actions.description: Look for hidden objects, creatures, or information using careful observation

standard_actions.name: Study
standard_actions.summary: Make an Intelligence (Arcana, History, Investigation, Nature, or Religion) check
standard_actions.description: Examine something closely to understand its properties, history, or function

standard_actions.name: Utilize
standard_actions.description: Interact with mundane items like tools, equipment, or environmental objects

improvised_actions.description: When you describe an action not detailed elsewhere in the rules, the Dungeon Master tells you whether that action is possible and what kind of D20 Test you need to make, if any
improvised_actions.dm_authority: The DM has final say on whether improvised actions are possible
improvised_actions.resolution: DM determines if a D20 Test is needed and sets the DC
examples: Attempting to swing from a chandelier
Trying to reason with a wild animal

additional_action_sources: Class features that grant unique actions
Feats that provide new action options
Spells that can be cast as actions

action_economy.description: Most characters can take one action per turn, plus movement and various bonus actions or reactions as available
action_economy.limitation: Generally limited to one action per turn unless specific features grant additional actions
action_economy.interaction: Actions work alongside bonus actions, reactions, and free object interactions to create tactical choices
